final-neighborhood-resource-guide-histor.label: Final Capstone: Build the Resource Guide History
final-neighborhood-resource-guide-histor.summary: Build a neighborhood resource guide from its first repository snapshot to a verified four-commit handoff.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Rename the Guide and Remove the Obsolete Note
Commit and Verify the Final Handoff
Create the First Guide Snapshot
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
Initialize `resource-guide/` on `main`, set the repository-only learner identity, stage the provided starting files, and commit `Start neighborhood resource guide`.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: The capstone begins with the same honest sequence as the course: initialize, identify, stage, commit, verify.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:
# Use the terminal for this Git task.
# The required starting files and repository state are already prepared.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
cd resource-guide
git init -b main
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git add README.md pages/
git commit -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"
git status --short
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions:
Initialize `resource-guide/` on `main`, set the repository-only learner identity, stage the provided starting files, and commit `Start neighborhood resource guide`.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

main_sh.content:
# Use the terminal for this Git task.
# The required starting files and repository state are already prepared.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf resource-guide
mkdir -p resource-guide
cd resource-guide
printf %s '# Neighborhood Resource Guide
' > README.md
cd ..
cd resource-guide
mkdir -p pages
printf %s 'Help desk closes at 6 PM.
' > pages/contact.md
cd ..
cd resource-guide
mkdir -p pages
printf %s 'Welcome to the neighborhood guide.
' > pages/welcome.md
cd ..

main_sh.content:
cd resource-guide
git init -b main
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git add README.md pages/
git commit -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"
git status --short

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

help.concept: A new page earns its own focused snapshot because it is a complete, reviewable addition.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions:
Create the supplied `pages/services.md` content, stage only that page, and commit `Add local services page`.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.

main_sh.content:
# Use the terminal for this Git task.
# The required starting files and repository state are already prepared.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf resource-guide
mkdir -p resource-guide
git -C resource-guide init -q -b main
git -C resource-guide config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C resource-guide config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
cd resource-guide
printf %s '# Neighborhood Resource Guide
' > README.md
cd ..
cd resource-guide
mkdir -p pages
printf %s 'Help desk closes at 6 PM.
' > pages/contact.md
cd ..
cd resource-guide
mkdir -p pages
printf %s 'Welcome to the neighborhood guide.
' > pages/welcome.md
cd ..
git -C resource-guide add -A
git -C resource-guide commit -q -m 'Start neighborhood resource guide'
cd resource-guide
mkdir -p pages
printf %s '- Food pantry
- Health clinic
- Job center
' > pages/services.md
cd ..

main_sh.content:
cd resource-guide
git add pages/services.md
git commit -m "Add local services page"
git status --short

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Add the Ignore Policy
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
Add `generated/` to `.gitignore`, stage the policy, and verify that `generated/preview.html` is ignored. Leave the policy staged for the final cleanup commit.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: The ignore policy is source-controlled, while generated preview output stays outside history.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:
# Use the terminal for this Git task.
# The required starting files and repository state are already prepared.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
cd resource-guide
printf "generated/\n" > .gitignore
git add .gitignore
git check-ignore generated/preview.html
git diff --staged --name-only
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions:

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
With `.gitignore` already staged, rename `pages/welcome.md` to `pages/getting-help.md`, remove `README.md`, and review all staged structural changes. Do not commit yet.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: Related cleanup actions can share one final snapshot when they serve the same handoff goal.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
cd resource-guide
git mv pages/welcome.md pages/getting-help.md
git rm README.md
git diff --staged --name-status

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Commit and Audit the Final Handoff
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
Commit the staged cleanup as `Prepare resource guide handoff`, then audit the one-line history, latest snapshot, tracked files, ignore rule, and clean status.

Type each command in the terminal and press Enter.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Read the repository state first, then use the Git command that changes only the requested boundary.

help.concept: A production-ready handoff is proven by its history, current tracked files, ignore behavior, and clean working state.
help.hint_1: Use the exact file or repository named in the task; avoid broad commands that include unrelated work.
help.hint_2: After the action, run the requested inspection command to confirm the final state.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:
# Use the terminal for this Git task.
# The required starting files and repository state are already prepared.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
cd resource-guide
git commit -m "Prepare resource guide handoff"
git log --oneline
git show --stat HEAD
git ls-files
git check-ignore generated/preview.html
git status --short

intro.title: Turn a Working Folder into a Trustworthy Handoff
intro.bodyMarkdown:
A neighborhood resource guide has useful content, outdated paths, and generated preview files. Over six cumulative steps, you will build a four-commit local history that another maintainer can understand.

The capstone brings the full course together: initialize the repository, configure local identity, add and update content in focused commits, ignore generated output, rename and remove tracked files safely, then verify the final timeline and clean state.

Nothing is uploaded. The result is a complete local repository whose history tells the story of the work.
